habitatVersion: post-v0.4 self-use loop on v0.4.0 Developer Preview baseline
scenario: Habitat self-use trace: Git mutation policy review before publishing a docs-only slice
fixtureState: Hazakura Habitat SwiftPM source checkout; generated report path and private local filesystem details omitted.
taskSummary: Run a post-v0.4 self-use observation, record a small behavior finding, verify it, then prepare a main-branch commit and push.
relevantHabitatSignals: Use SwiftPM because project files point to it.
Ask before Git/GitHub workspace, history, branch, or remote mutations; see `command_policy.md`.
`command_policy.md` Review First includes `git clean` and `git reset --hard` with `git_mutation` reasons.
`command_policy.md` Ask First annotates `git add`, `git commit`, and `git push` with `git_mutation`.

askFirst: Git/GitHub workspace, history, branch, or remote mutations
doNot: dependency resolution as a preflight substitute
reviewFirst: Open command_policy.md before staging, committing, or pushing the observation slice.

withoutHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: After a docs-only observation, stage and commit the changed files directly as routine repository cleanup.
withoutHabitatContext.risk: Routine publish steps can become broad Git mutations without first checking whether generated policy separates local workspace mutation from remote repository action.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Read command_policy.md before Git mutation, verify the slice, inspect the diff, then stage only the intended documentation and evidence files before committing and pushing.

evidenceNormalizationImplication: No normalized evidence slice is justified. The v0.4 reason-code and command-reason output was sufficient to make Git publication a review-first, scoped action.

verdict.reason: Rendered `git_mutation` guidance changed the publish path from broad routine staging into policy review, verification, diff inspection, explicit-file staging, commit, and push.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Keep observing Git publication decisions through existing PolicyFinding command reasons before adding a broader evidence-normalization layer.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If agents skip policy review before `git add`, `git commit`, or `git push`, tighten the short-context Git/GitHub reminder or promote machine-readable review-first entries in examples.
